metadata.archetype_justification: The application owns persistent state (SQLite/PostgreSQL databases), exposes CRUD API endpoints for movies, collections, and configuration, and manages entity lifecycle (movie status, monitored state). Classified as stateful-crud.

top_gaps.title: Managed Compute
top_gaps.gap: No cloud compute infrastructure — self-hosted desktop application with no container or serverless deployment
top_gaps.impact: Prevents elastic scaling, automated recovery, and managed operations

top_gaps.title: Infrastructure as Code Coverage
top_gaps.gap: No IaC — all infrastructure would be manually configured
top_gaps.impact: No reproducible deployments, no environment consistency, no disaster recovery

top_gaps.title: Secrets Management
top_gaps.gap: Database credentials passed via environment variables without rotation or encryption
top_gaps.impact: Credentials exposed in process environment; no automated rotation

top_gaps.title: Audit Logging
top_gaps.gap: No CloudTrail or cloud audit logging
top_gaps.impact: No compliance audit trail, no forensic analysis capability

top_gaps.title: Distributed Tracing
top_gaps.gap: No distributed tracing — NLog provides basic logging but no trace propagation
top_gaps.impact: No cross-request correlation; debugging production issues is guesswork

quick_agent_wins.name: API-aware Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: API docs exist (APP-Q5 >= 2) — OpenAPI 3.0.4 spec at src/Radarr.Api.V3/openapi.json
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that discovers and invokes Radarr's REST API endpoints as tools — managing movies, triggering searches, monitoring queues
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: The OpenAPI spec is already comprehensive. Agent could be implemented directly against the existing API surface.

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists (README, SECURITY.md, OpenAPI spec, 140 migration files documenting schema evolution)
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: A knowledge agent answering developer questions about the Radarr codebase, API contracts, database schema, and configuration
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Index the OpenAPI spec, migration files, and code documentation.

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: CI/CD pipeline exists (INF-Q11 >= 2) — Azure Pipelines with multi-stage build, test, and analysis
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that triggers builds, checks test results, manages release versions, and monitors pipeline health
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Ensure Azure DevOps API tokens are available for agent access.

phases: Phase 2: Add IaC for EKS cluster, Aurora PostgreSQL, networking
Phase 3: Extract high-value services (Notification Service, Download Client Manager, Media File Processor)

evaluations.reason: stateful-crud with no cloud workflows to evaluate in self-hosted desktop application
has_deployed_workload=false — no cloud deployment artifacts exist
has_at_rest_data_surface=false — no deployed cloud data-at-rest surface
Modern cloud-native language (.NET 8/C# with ASP.NET Core 8.0 and AWS SDK v3 coverage) and TypeScript 5.7.2/React 18.3.1 frontend
No stored procedures, triggers, or proprietary SQL. All business logic in application layer with standard DDL migrations.

findings.title: No managed compute — self-hosted application
findings.description: No managed compute infrastructure exists. The application is distributed as self-contained .NET 8 executables for multiple platforms and runs as a self-hosted service. No ECS, EKS, Lambda, Fargate, or EC2 resources are defined.
findings.gap: All compute is self-hosted with no cloud deployment model. No elastic scaling, automated recovery, or managed operations.
findings.recommendation: Containerize the application and deploy to EKS (preferred). Create a Dockerfile using the .NET 8 runtime base image.

findings.title: All databases self-managed (SQLite/PostgreSQL)
findings.description: SQLite is the default embedded database. PostgreSQL is supported optionally via environment variables. Neither uses a managed database service.
findings.gap: No managed database service. SQLite has no failover, limited concurrency. PostgreSQL is self-managed with manual patching.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Aurora PostgreSQL (preferred). The application already supports PostgreSQL natively via FluentMigrator.

findings.title: No managed messaging or streaming infrastructure
findings.description: In-process event aggregator and SignalR for browser notifications only. No SQS, SNS, EventBridge, or external messaging.
findings.gap: No async messaging for cross-service communication. All operations are synchronous in-process calls.
findings.recommendation: Introduce EventBridge (preferred) for cross-service events and SQS for durable command queues when decomposing.

findings.title: No cloud network security defined
findings.description: No VPC, subnets, security groups, or network segmentation. Application binds directly to port 7878 with basic access control.
findings.gap: No cloud network security. Service would need private subnets, security groups, and proper segmentation when deployed.
findings.recommendation: Define VPC with private subnets. Use security groups for least-privilege access. Deploy API Gateway as public entry point.

findings.title: No API Gateway or load balancer
findings.description: REST API exposed directly on port 7878 with no external entry point providing throttling, auth offload, or request validation.
findings.gap: Services exposed directly with no gateway or load balancer.
findings.recommendation: Deploy API Gateway (preferred) with throttling and request validation. Use existing OpenAPI spec to configure routes.

findings.title: No auto-scaling configured
findings.description: Single-process application with no scaling capability. No ASG, ECS service scaling, or HPA.
findings.gap: All capacity statically provisioned. No ability to respond to traffic spikes.
findings.recommendation: Configure HPA on EKS based on CPU/memory and custom metrics when containerized.

findings.title: No automated backup configuration
findings.description: SQLite databases stored as local files with no backup automation. PostgreSQL relies on manual backup procedures.
findings.gap: No backup configuration found. Data loss event would wipe application state.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Aurora PostgreSQL with automated backups, configurable retention, PITR, and cross-region replication.

findings.title: Zero Infrastructure as Code coverage
findings.description: No Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, Helm charts, or Kustomize files exist. All infrastructure would be manually provisioned.
findings.gap: No IaC — all infrastructure created manually. No reproducible deployments.
findings.recommendation: Create IaC using CDK or Terraform for EKS cluster, Aurora PostgreSQL, VPC/networking, API Gateway.

findings.title: CI/CD covers build and test but no deployment
findings.description: Comprehensive CI/CD via Azure Pipelines with build, test (unit + integration + automation), SonarCloud analysis, and coverage. No deployment stages.
findings.gap: Pipeline produces artifacts but does not deploy. No automated deployment to any environment.
findings.recommendation: Extend pipeline with deployment stages once IaC and containerization are in place.

findings.title: Tightly-coupled monolith with pervasive shared state
findings.description: Single deployable unit with all business logic in NzbDrone.Core. No clear service boundaries. Cross-domain coupling throughout.
findings.gap: Cannot independently scale, deploy, or evolve subsystems. Team velocity limited by monolith coupling.
findings.recommendation: Containerize first (Conditional approach), then progressively extract services via Strangler Fig.

findings.title: Primarily synchronous communication with limited async
findings.description: In-process event aggregator provides fire-and-forget within the app. External integrations are synchronous HTTP. Background tasks provide some async.
findings.gap: No managed async messaging for cross-boundary operations. External integrations are synchronous HTTP.
findings.recommendation: Introduce EventBridge for cross-service events and SQS for durable queues when decomposing.

findings.title: Inconsistent background job processing
findings.description: Custom command executor for background operations. Long-running operations block the command queue sequentially. No external job infrastructure.
findings.gap: No external job infrastructure. Long-running operations run in-process with no timeout management.
findings.recommendation: Implement SQS + Step Functions for multi-step operations. Existing command pattern maps to SQS handlers.

findings.title: API versioning applied consistently but lacks governance
findings.description: Consistent /api/v3/ URL versioning across all endpoints. No backward compatibility guarantees or deprecation policy documented.
findings.gap: Only single version active. No deprecation timeline or compatibility guarantees documented.
findings.recommendation: Document API versioning policy including backward compatibility commitments and deprecation timeline.

findings.title: Environment variables for endpoints without dynamic discovery
findings.description: PostgreSQL endpoint via env vars. Other service endpoints stored in application database. No dynamic discovery mechanism.
findings.gap: No dynamic discovery. Adding or moving a service requires manual configuration change.
findings.recommendation: Leverage Kubernetes service discovery on EKS. Use VPC Lattice for cross-service communication.

findings.title: Unstructured data on local filesystem
findings.description: Media covers, posters, metadata stored on local filesystem. No S3 or managed object storage. ImageSharp for local image processing.
findings.gap: Data in local file system with limited accessibility. Not in managed object storage.
findings.recommendation: Migrate media covers and metadata to S3. Use S3 File Gateway for compatibility with file-path access patterns.

findings.title: Unified data access layer mostly centralized with minor gaps
findings.description: A mostly centralized data access layer exists using the BasicRepository<TModel> generic repository pattern with Dapper. All database operations go through repositories that inherit from BasicRepository. Custom SQL builders (SqlBuilder.cs, WhereBuilder.cs) provide a consistent query construction pattern. Platform-specific builders exist for SQLite and PostgreSQL.
findings.gap: Mostly centralized but some direct database access may exist in auxiliary code paths. The custom SQL builder pattern is consistent within the repository layer but requires knowledge of both SQLite and PostgreSQL SQL dialects.
findings.recommendation: Maintain the centralized repository pattern. When migrating to Aurora PostgreSQL, the PostgreSQL-specific WhereBuilderPostgres.cs provides the correct query path — remove SQLite-specific code paths post-migration.

findings.title: PostgreSQL version not pinned in application
findings.description: SQLite version pinned via NuGet. PostgreSQL version uncontrolled — connects to whatever instance is configured. CI tests against Postgres 14/15.
findings.gap: PostgreSQL version not pinned or managed. No documented version-update procedure.
findings.recommendation: Pin engine version in IaC when migrating to Aurora PostgreSQL. Document upgrade procedures.

findings.title: No CloudTrail or cloud audit logging
findings.description: HTTP requests logged locally via NLog. No immutable audit trail, no centralized log collection, no compliance-grade logging.
findings.gap: No CloudTrail. Local log files are mutable, not centrally collected, not suitable for compliance.
findings.recommendation: Enable CloudTrail with log file validation and S3 Object Lock. Forward application logs to CloudWatch Logs.

findings.title: API key authentication without token-based auth
findings.description: Static API key via X-Api-Key header or query param. Simple string comparison. No OAuth2/JWT, no per-user identity, no token rotation.
findings.gap: Static credential auth without per-request identity or token rotation.
findings.recommendation: Implement Cognito user pools or OAuth2/JWT via API Gateway authorizers.

findings.title: Self-managed authentication with no external IdP
findings.description: User credentials stored locally. Password hashing via KeyDerivation. No integration with Cognito, Okta, OIDC, or SAML.
findings.gap: Application manages its own authentication entirely. No SSO, no federation, no centralized identity.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with Cognito for centralized identity. Support OIDC/SAML federation for SSO.

findings.title: Credentials in environment variables without rotation
findings.description: PostgreSQL credentials via plain env vars. CI/CD secrets in pipeline variables. API keys in application database. No Secrets Manager.
findings.gap: Production credentials in plain environment variables without rotation. No dedicated secrets management.
findings.recommendation: Integrate AWS Secrets Manager for database credentials. Configure automated rotation.

findings.title: SonarCloud analysis but minimal dependency scanning
findings.description: SonarCloud SAST and StyleCop in CI. Dependabot only tracks devcontainers. No NuGet or npm vulnerability scanning.
findings.gap: No dependency vulnerability scanning for NuGet/npm in pipeline. No container scanning.
findings.recommendation: Enable Dependabot for NuGet and npm. When containerized, use hardened base images and ECR scanning.

findings.title: SAST present but no dependency vulnerability scanning in pipeline
findings.description: SonarCloud for backend and frontend SAST. Code coverage via coverlet. No dotnet list package --vulnerable or yarn audit in pipeline.
findings.gap: SAST present but no dependency scanning. No security gate blocking on critical findings.
findings.recommendation: Add dotnet list package --vulnerable and yarn audit. Configure SonarCloud quality gates.

findings.title: No distributed tracing
findings.description: NLog with request sequence IDs (per-instance counters). No OpenTelemetry, X-Ray, or trace header propagation. Sentry for errors only.
findings.gap: No distributed tracing. No trace ID propagation across service boundaries.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenTelemetry SDK for .NET. Export traces to X-Ray when deployed to AWS.

findings.title: No SLO definitions
findings.description: No formal SLOs for API latency, availability, or error rates. Health checks monitor component health but not service-level objectives.
findings.gap: No SLOs — no formal definition of acceptable service levels.
findings.recommendation: Define SLOs for critical journeys: API latency p99 < 500ms, availability 99.9%. Implement with CloudWatch.

findings.title: Infrastructure metrics only — no published business metrics
findings.description: Health check results and command status tracked internally. No external metrics publishing. No CloudWatch custom metrics.
findings.gap: No published business outcome metrics (movies added, downloads completed, import success rate).
findings.recommendation: Publish business metrics to CloudWatch. Create dashboards for operational visibility.

findings.title: No alerting or anomaly detection
findings.description: Sentry for crash reporting. Health check failures visible only in UI. No external alerting for error rates or latency anomalies.
findings.gap: No alerting configured. Health check failures only visible in application UI.
findings.recommendation: Configure CloudWatch anomaly detection on error rates and latency. Set up SNS alarms.

findings.title: Direct-to-production self-update with no staged rollout
findings.description: Self-update mechanism replaces binary in-place. No blue/green, canary, or rolling deployment. No health verification during update.
findings.gap: Direct-to-production replacement with no staged rollout or automated rollback.
findings.recommendation: Implement rolling deployments with health check gates on EKS. Progress to blue/green via CodeDeploy.

findings.title: Integration tests run in CI but gaps in deployment testing
findings.description: Integration tests against full stack on multiple platforms with PostgreSQL 14/15. UI automation tests. Run in Azure Pipelines.
findings.gap: Tests cover correctness but not deployment scenarios (container health, startup probes, graceful shutdown).
findings.recommendation: Extend integration tests to cover container deployment scenarios and contract tests.

findings.title: No incident response automation or runbooks
findings.description: No runbooks, no self-healing automation, no SSM documents. Health checks detect issues but have no remediation capability.
findings.gap: No runbooks — incident response is entirely ad hoc.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for common scenarios. Implement SSM Automation documents for self-healing on AWS.

findings.title: No defined observability ownership
findings.description: No per-service dashboards, no alarms with named owners, no CODEOWNERS for observability assets.
findings.gap: No observability ownership — monitoring is reactive and fragmented.
findings.recommendation: Establish observability ownership: CODEOWNERS for configs, per-domain dashboards, alarm ownership.

findings.title: No resource tagging governance
findings.description: No AWS resources defined (no IaC). No tagging standards, cost allocation tags, or ownership tags exist.
findings.gap: No tags on resources. No tagging standard. Cannot track costs or identify ownership.
findings.recommendation: Establish tagging standard (Environment, Service, Team, CostCenter) before creating IaC.

pathways.name: Move to Cloud Native
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: APP-Q2=1 (monolith), INF-Q1=1 (no managed compute), APP-Q3=2, APP-Q4=2

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1=1 (no managed compute), no container definitions found in repository

triggering_questions.note: No Dockerfile or container definitions exist

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4=4 (no stored procedures); no commercial database engines detected (SQLite and PostgreSQL are both open source)

triggering_questions.note: No stored procedures — no commercial DB coupling

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No commercial database engines detected. SQLite and PostgreSQL are both open source. DATA-Q4=4 — no proprietary SQL.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q2=1 (all databases self-managed: SQLite embedded + PostgreSQL via env vars)

immediate_actions: Update connection string to use Secrets Manager

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No data processing workloads exist. Contextual guard prevents trigger — no streaming, ETL, data pipeline, or analytics artifacts found.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No data processing workloads exist in the repository. The application is a CRUD-focused movie manager with no streaming, ETL, or analytics requirements.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q10=1 (no IaC), OPS-Q5=1 (no deployment strategy), OPS-Q6=3 (integration tests exist but gaps)

triggering_questions.note: CI/CD exists but no deployment stages

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. The context ('Movie collection manager') does not contain AI-related signal terms.
